title: senecio papagonius
intro: Senecio papagonius, also known as Jacob's ladder, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant is rich in saponins, which contribute to its health benefits, including lowering cholesterol levels and acting as an antioxidant. Jacob's ladder can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its use should be carefully monitored due to potential toxicity at high doses.
